history.hypothesis: SPY exhibits momentum phases that can be captured by combining ROC for momentum detection, EMA for trend filtering, and volume confirmation to avoid false signals. ATR-based trailing stops will control drawdown by adapting to SPY's volatility regime. This combination should achieve Sharpe > 1.5 while keeping drawdown under 25% by only entering when multiple conditions align.

history.hypothesis: SPY exhibits persistent momentum trends where ROC crossovers capture momentum shifts effectively. Combining ROC zero-cross entries with EMA trend filter (price > EMA confirms uptrend) and volume confirmation (above average volume validates institutional participation) should generate reliable signals. Previous attempts failed due to ATR API errors and overly restrictive conditions - this version uses only well-tested indicators (ROC, EMA, SMA for volume) with relaxed volume multiplier (0.5-1.2 range) to ensure minimum 5 trades while maintaining quality through confluence. The exit on ROC crossing below zero captures momentum exhaustion without needing complex trailing stops.
